pr.title: Split sdata2 through all the fighters
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved the large monolithic fighter-related .sdata2 block out of asm/sdata2.s and distributed those small-data constants into the corresponding fighter assembly files under asm/melee/ft. The PR also relocated func_8015BC88 from ftbosslib.s into ftcrazyhand.s, consistent with putting code/data closer to the fighter file that owns the address range or references. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so rationale is inferred from the title and diff structure.
what_changed: Deleted 2776 lines from the central asm/sdata2.s file.
Added .section .sdata2 blocks to fighter assembly files such as ftclink.s, ftdrmario.s, ftfalco.s, ftfox.s, ftgamewatch.s, ftkirby.s, ftmasterhand.s, ftcrazyhand.s, and many others.
Preserved existing generated labels such as lbl_804D9DA0, lbl_804DA050, lbl_804DA1F4, and related .global declarations while moving them into fighter-local assembly files.
Kept constants as raw .4byte values, including common float/double patterns such as 0x00000000, 0x3F800000, 0xBF800000, 0x43300000/0x80000000, and pi-related values like 0x3FF921FB/0x54442D18.
Moved func_8015BC88 out of asm/melee/ft/ftbosslib.s and into asm/melee/ft/ftcrazyhand.s, according to the diff excerpt.
Added small .sdata2 blocks to clone or related fighter files with only a pair of constants, for example ftclink.s, ftdrmario.s, ftemblem.s, ftfalco.s, ftgigakoopa.s, and ftpichu.s.
Touched 34 files with approximately 2954 added lines and 2819 deleted lines, making the net effect mostly a relocation/split rather than new behavior.
smart_moves: Split constants by fighter file instead of keeping all fighter .sdata2 in one global asm/sdata2.s bucket, which makes future decompilation of individual fighters easier to localize.
Preserved labels and exact raw data encodings, minimizing risk of changing references or binary layout while reorganizing section ownership.
Placed .section .sdata2 blocks after existing .text/.data content in each assembly file, keeping each translation unit self-contained for its code and small-data constants.
Handled both large fighter files and tiny clone/helper files, avoiding a partial split where some fighter constants would remain stranded in the monolithic sdata2 file.
Relocated func_8015BC88 alongside ftcrazyhand content while keeping the function body byte-for-byte in the diff excerpt, suggesting ownership correction rather than behavioral editing.
decomp_lessons: When splitting monolithic assembly data sections, preserve original labels, order, alignment, and raw .4byte encodings first; semantic naming can come later after matching is stable.
For fighter decomp work, colocating .sdata2 constants with the fighter assembly/source that references them reduces cross-file noise and makes later C conversion easier.
Small data referenced through r2/sda21, such as lbl_804DA1F4@sda21(r2), must remain in .sdata2 and keep address-sensitive labels intact during reorganization.
Large mechanical splits can be useful groundwork PRs even without decompiling functions, because they establish file ownership boundaries for later matching.
A monolithic asm/sdata2.s can hide ownership; distributing constants by module exposes which fighter files still depend on raw constants.
Function ownership may need to be corrected during data splitting if nearby code/data address ranges reveal that a function belongs with another fighter file.
naming_conventions: Retained generated symbol names in the form lbl_804Dxxxx for data labels.
Retained generated function names in the form func_80xxxxxx, including func_8015BC88, func_8015BD20, func_8014947C, and related symbols visible in the excerpt.
Fighter assembly files follow the asm/melee/ft/ft<character>.s convention, including ftfox.s, ftkirby.s, ftcrazyhand.s, ftmasterhand.s, ftdonkey_1.s, and ftdonkey_2.s.
The PR did not introduce semantic names for constants or functions; it preserved anonymous decomp labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used per-file .section .sdata2 declarations to move constants while keeping them addressable through existing global labels.
Kept constants in hexadecimal .4byte form rather than converting to float literals, which avoids assembler formatting or precision surprises.
Maintained 8-byte pairs for double constants and integer-to-double conversion patterns, for example 0x43300000 followed by 0x80000000.
Preserved .global declarations for every moved label so existing references from assembly continue to resolve.
Relocated assembly without rewriting instruction sequences, which is the safest tactic for layout-preserving organization work.
Included padding/paired zero words where present, such as two-word entries and explicit 0x00000000 fillers, rather than collapsing constants.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided context.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes can be extracted from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert fighter .sdata2 labels to named C constants after this split.
Search for remaining references to asm/sdata2.s after PR 192 to identify non-fighter small-data still centralized.
Inspect whether func_8015BC88 later receives a semantic Crazy Hand or boss-related name.
Compare fighter linker/map ordering around .sdata2 after the split to confirm constants stayed in expected address order.
Look for subsequent PRs touching ftcrazyhand.s lbl_804DA050 through lbl_804DA1F4.
